title: GitHub Actions cost skips need a safety boundary
summary: When optimizing GitHub Actions spend, agents may add path filters, job-level conditions, broad concurrency cancellation, or tighter timeouts without checking whether the skipped work is a required status check, a safety guard, or a source/config boundary check. The safe first move is to separate cost evidence from safety semantics before editing workflows.
ai_value: Helps coding and operations agents reduce CI spend without making required checks disappear, inverting label/scope guards, missing config-triggered boundary risks, or setting timeouts from intuition instead of run-duration evidence.

contexts: an agent is asked to reduce GitHub Actions spend, runner minutes, workflow frequency, or CI cost
a proposed workflow edit adds or tightens paths, paths-ignore, branches, job-level if conditions, concurrency.cancel-in-progress, or timeout-minutes
a repository has required status checks, branch protection, rulesets, safety guard workflows, import-boundary checks, or release/deploy-adjacent CI
a workflow appears cheap per run but runs on many pull requests or push events
a safety guard normally exits early because the changed files, labels, or PR scope do not require deeper validation

tools.name: GitHub Actions
tools.version_range: current public GitHub Actions semantics as of 2026-05-10

trigger_signals.description: The task mentions high Actions spend, runner minutes, budget exhaustion, docs-only skips, reducing workflow frequency, or cheaper CI.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Do not edit triggers yet. First classify whether the cost driver is runner SKU, run volume, duration, artifacts/storage, or repeated superseded runs.

trigger_signals.description: A proposed patch makes a workflow or job skip through paths, paths-ignore, branches, a job-level if, a reduced schedule, or concurrency.cancel-in-progress.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Before applying the patch, check required-check behavior and whether the skipped job is part of a safety boundary.

trigger_signals.description: The skipped or conditionally skipped job is a guard for labels, scope, import boundaries, security posture, release safety, dependency changes, or project architecture rules.
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Treat the skip as semantics-changing until the guard's purpose and false-negative cases are understood.

trigger_signals.description: A timeout is shortened because recent runs seemed fast, without checking recent successful p90/max duration and variance.
trigger_signals.pattern: timeout-minutes|recent runs are fast|shorter timeout|runner timeout
trigger_signals.agent_interpretation: Use observed run-duration evidence and headroom rather than intuition before tightening timeout-minutes.

negative_signals.description: The workflow is not a required status check, has no safety or release semantics, and a skipped run is acceptable for the repository's documented policy.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: The trap targets unsafe or unexamined skips. Optional advisory workflows can often use path or schedule reductions after ordinary review.

negative_signals.description: A required-check aggregator or explicit no-op success path preserves the required check for excluded changes.
negative_signals.pattern: required check aggregator|noop success|stub job|always reports success for skipped paths
negative_signals.why_it_matters: The required-check problem is about making the check disappear or remain pending. An intentional replacement signal can make path filtering safe.

negative_signals.description: The job-level condition only skips work after an unconditional safety wrapper has validated labels, scope, required files, and false-negative cases.
negative_signals.pattern: validated before skip|guard condition inside validation|unconditional wrapper
negative_signals.why_it_matters: Skipping expensive work after the safety decision is different from skipping the safety decision itself.

negative_signals.description: Recent successful run history shows the tighter timeout remains above observed max or high percentile with explicit headroom, and the next main run confirms it.
negative_signals.why_it_matters: Timeout changes can be reasonable when they are evidence-based and verified, not guessed.

common_wrong_assumptions: If a docs-only pull request does not need full CI, it is always safe for the CI workflow not to start.
A job that normally no-ops can be skipped at the job level without changing the meaning of the safety guard.
Source-only path filters are enough for import-boundary, dependency, or security checks even when config files can change semantics.
A short timeout is safe because recent runs usually finish quickly, even without checking p90, max, variance, and the next main run.
Expensive runner types or matrix builds are always the cause of high Actions cost; run volume, cancellation policy, artifacts, and repeated reruns are secondary.
A concurrency group is harmless if it cancels old runs, even when the group name can collide across workflows, branches, or release jobs.

first_checks.check: Separate price multiplier from volume: inspect billing/usage, recent workflow run counts, runner types, durations, artifact/cache storage, and rerun/cancellation patterns before blaming a specific workflow or runner SKU.
first_checks.why: Cost can come from per-minute rates, run volume, storage, or repeated superseded runs; the safest optimization depends on the actual cost driver.
first_checks.command: Review GitHub billing/usage and recent Actions run history before editing workflow YAML.

first_checks.check: Check branch protection, rulesets, and required status checks before skipping an entire workflow with paths, paths-ignore, branches, or commit-message skip behavior.
first_checks.why: GitHub documents that workflows skipped by branch/path/message filters can leave associated checks pending, blocking protected-branch merges if those checks are required.
first_checks.command: Inspect repository branch protection/rulesets and required status check names, using the project's normal read-only settings/API path.

first_checks.check: For job-level if conditions on safety guards, identify what the guard is supposed to catch and whether absence or misuse of a label/scope marker is part of the risk.
first_checks.why: If unlabeled, mislabeled, or unexpected-scope pull requests are a failure mode, a job-level skip can make the guard invisible exactly when it should speak.

first_checks.check: For path filters on boundary, security, dependency, or import checks, include config surfaces that change semantics: package manifests, lockfiles, build/test config, tsconfig, lint/boundary config, module resolution config, action definitions, and workflow files.
first_checks.why: A source-only filter can miss changes that alter dependency resolution, imports, checks, or CI behavior without touching source files.

first_checks.check: Before reducing timeout-minutes, sample recent successful job durations and set a threshold above observed max or high percentile with headroom; verify on the next main/default-branch run.
first_checks.why: Timeouts are safety and reliability limits. A guessed timeout can convert slow-but-valid checks into flaky failures or hide real regression timing.
first_checks.command: Review recent job execution times in GitHub Actions run history or job usage views.

first_checks.check: Smoke-test an excluded-path pull request or dry-run equivalent and record which workflows start, skip, or report a replacement success signal.
first_checks.why: The observable result confirms whether the cost-saving edit preserved mergeability and required-check semantics.

decision_rules.condition: A proposed path, branch, or commit-message skip may affect a workflow whose check is required by branch protection or rulesets.
decision_rules.then: Do not make the required workflow disappear. Either keep it running, add a required-check aggregator/no-op success path that preserves the required signal, or route the required-check policy change through the repository's review/approval path.

decision_rules.condition: A job-level if would skip a guard whose purpose includes catching absent, wrong, or unexpected labels/scope/configuration.
decision_rules.then: Keep the safety guard visible, perform the cheap validation unconditionally, and only skip expensive subwork after the guard has made an explicit pass/skip/fail decision.

decision_rules.condition: A boundary, dependency, security, or import-check workflow is filtered only to source directories while config or dependency files can alter the checked semantics.
decision_rules.then: Include package manifests, lockfiles, build/test/lint/boundary config, module resolution config, action definitions, and workflow files that can change the check's meaning.

decision_rules.condition: The cost driver is repeated superseded pull-request runs and the workflow is not a release/deploy/ordered-state operation that must finish every run.
decision_rules.then: Prefer canceling superseded PR runs with a workflow-specific concurrency group, while avoiding group-name collisions across workflows, branches, release jobs, or environments that require ordered completion.

decision_rules.condition: A shorter timeout is proposed but recent duration evidence has not been sampled.
decision_rules.then: Collect recent successful job durations, choose a timeout above observed max or high percentile with headroom, and verify on the next main/default-branch run before claiming the optimization is safe.

decision_rules.condition: The workflow is optional, non-safety-critical, not required for protected branches, and the excluded paths cannot affect the workflow's semantics.
decision_rules.then: Proceed with normal workflow optimization review, while documenting which required-check and safety-boundary checks made the skip acceptable.

do_not: Do not make a required workflow disappear without preserving a required success signal or changing the requirement through the repository's approved path.
Do not skip label, scope, boundary, or security safety guards at the job level when that makes unlabeled, mislabeled, or config-only changes invisible.
Do not filter boundary checks only to source files when manifests, lockfiles, build config, lint config, workflow files, or module-resolution config can change the boundary.
Do not set timeout-minutes from intuition; use observed successful duration evidence and follow-up verification.
Do not use a broad concurrency group that can cancel unrelated workflows, release jobs, deploy jobs, or branches that must complete independently.
Do not report cost savings from workflow edits until required-check behavior and the next main/default-branch run have been checked.

agent_directives.preferred_next_step: Classify the workflow's cost driver and safety role, check required status checks/rulesets, then design the smallest cost reduction that preserves required-check signals and guard semantics.

risk.notes: Read-only cost analysis and local workflow drafting are low risk, but mutating shared CI, required status checks, release/deploy workflows, billing controls, or repository rules can affect mergeability, safety, availability, or cost. Route those mutations through the repository's review and approval path.

sources.excerpt: A de-identified CI cost-reduction review found that a job-level safety-guard skip could invert a label/scope guard and that boundary-check path filters needed config surfaces.
sources.supports: Provides the originating public-safe observation and accepted mitigation pattern. This remains a single-session candidate rather than canonical guidance.

sources.excerpt: GitHub documents that workflows skipped by branch filtering, path filtering, or commit messages can leave associated checks pending and block protected-branch merging when those checks are required.
sources.supports: Supports the required-check safety boundary for paths, paths-ignore, branch filters, and commit-message skips.

sources.excerpt: GitHub documents that Actions usage is charged to the repository owner, and that minutes, storage, and runner type affect billing.
sources.supports: Supports separating price multiplier, volume, storage, and ownership before choosing a cost optimization.

sources.excerpt: GitHub documents how to view job execution time and billable job execution minutes for workflow runs.
sources.supports: Supports sampling observed job durations before setting tighter timeouts or claiming savings.

sources.excerpt: GitHub documents job and step timeout-minutes semantics, including positive-integer job timeouts and the default job timeout.
sources.supports: Supports treating timeout changes as workflow semantics that need evidence and verification.

sources.excerpt: GitHub documents concurrency groups, cancel-in-progress behavior, and the need to use unique group names to avoid canceling unrelated in-progress or pending jobs.
sources.supports: Supports using scoped concurrency cancellation carefully rather than broad cancellation as a blanket cost cut.

sources.excerpt: The trap should not match optional advisory workflows whose skipped paths cannot affect safety, required-check, release, or semantic boundary behavior.
sources.supports: Defines a public-safe false-positive boundary for this candidate record.
